title: Story
description: A unit of work defined by outcome + executable acceptance + rebuild guidance. The authoritative contract; all story YAMLs are validated against this at parse time.

id.description: Unique positive integer. Must match the filename (stories/<id>.yml).

title.description: Short human-readable title. Label only — not part of the proof hash.

outcome.description: Plain-English value statement. Why this story exists and what it delivers. Must be expressible as one sentence without conjunctions (no 'and', 'also', 'as well as'); otherwise split the story.

status.description: Lifecycle state. `proposed` is written by humans (story authoring) or the story-writer (creation/curation). The `proposed → under_construction` transition is written by `build-rust` at start-of-implementation as the first commit before any code edits, per `agents/build/build-rust/contract.yml` and the carve-out in `.claude/hooks/build_rust_guard.py`. The story-writer may also write `under_construction` when auto-reverting a previously-`healthy` story under the defects-amend-the-owning-story loop. `healthy` is written only by `agentic uat` on a Pass verdict. `unhealthy` is computed by the dashboard from evidence and is never written to disk. `retired` is a terminal off-tree state.

patterns.description: IDs of patterns (slugs) this story applies. Each must reference an existing patterns/<id>.yml file. Defaults to []. The story-writer always writes this field explicitly, even when empty.

assets.description: Repo-root-relative paths to asset YAMLs this story declares as relevant cross-corpus concepts (principles, definitions, guidelines). Loader validates each path resolves to an existing file at directory-load time per ADR-0007 decision 3. Field is opt-in: absent and empty are both accepted and observably equivalent post-load (matching the patterns: field's posture). Reciprocity with each referenced asset's current_consumers: is a corpus-level invariant per ADR-0007 decision 4. See docs/decisions/0007-stories-consume-assets.md.

tests.description: One or more test entries. A story has a one-to-many relationship with tests; each entry has its own justification.

file.description: Path to a test file (relative to repo root). Must exist at verify time (not at parse time — stories may be authored before their tests are written, in which case the implementing agent creates the file). Must be referenced by exactly one story (1-to-1 binding). Test files live under crates/*/tests/ or scripts/verify/.

justification.description: What THIS specific test proves and why it's sufficient for its scope. Each test in the array gets its own justification — no aggregate justification.

uat.description: Prose walkthrough a UAT agent or human executes end-to-end. Must include cleanup. Free-form; serves as guidance rather than a strict script.

guidance.description: Non-obvious technical context needed to rebuild this component from scratch. Include only what an agent would NOT independently derive from outcome + acceptance alone. Length is unconstrained — split the story if it covers multiple objectives, not because guidance grew.

depends_on.description: IDs of other stories that must reach `healthy` before this story can be marked `healthy`. Cycles are rejected at load time.

related_files.description: Paths or globs (relative to repo root) whose change since the last UAT pass commit marks this story stale. Absent or empty means no file-based staleness signal — the story is healthy as long as the UAT pass row exists and tests are not red.

build_config.description: Optional build configuration. Contains max_inner_loop_iterations (positive integer) and models (array of strings). Absent means no opinion; when present, used by story 19's runtime.

superseded_by.description: Optional edge pointing at the successor when a retired story was superseded. Absent means terminal retirement. Only meaningful when status: retired.

retired_reason.description: Optional prose documenting why this story was retired. Absent is valid when no reason is known or recorded.
